title: Migration-agent recipe
description: A reusable, structured knowledge entry describing how to handle a specific ComfyUI nodeType + model pattern on Intel XPU. Recipes live in recipes/ and are auto-injected by Step 04 source audit. See docs/evolution-and-memory-design.md §5.3.

nodeType.description: ComfyUI node class name this recipe applies to, e.g. CLIPLoader, VAELoader.

modelPattern.description: Glob matched against model filenames. Absent means 'all models for this nodeType'.

providesEnumValues.description: Enum widget values this package injects into the host nodeType's dropdowns (e.g. RES4LYF adds ['res_2s','res_2m','bong_tangent']). Used to detect implicit package dependencies: a workflow using one of these values needs this package installed on the target to stay identical to source.

enumSlots.description: The host nodeType's enum slots the values above belong to, e.g. ['sampler_name','scheduler'].

packageRepo.description: Git repo URL to clone into custom_nodes/ to install this package (for enum-value/custom-node dependencies).

xpuSupport.description: native=works out of box; patched=needs source patch; cpu_offload=run on CPU; unsupported=no known path.

patchFile.description: Repo-relative path to the patch file under patches/.

patchTarget.description: File(s) and symbol(s) the patch modifies, e.g. 'src/optimization/memory_manager.py::get_vram_usage, src/interfaces/video_upscaler.py'. Helps ast-grep and LLM adaptation locate code during patch adaptation.

validationCommand.description: Shell command (run from the package root) that exits 0 if the patch achieved its goal. Used by the patch adaptation protocol to verify success.

baseVersion.description: Package commit the patch was authored against, e.g. 'numz/ComfyUI-SeedVR2_VideoUpscaler@4490bd1'. Used to detect version drift and pick the adaptation layer.

knownIssues.description: Concrete failure signatures, with root cause if known.

workarounds.description: Ordered steps the agent should try, most-preferred first.

action.description: Concrete instruction, e.g. 'set CLIPLoader device=cpu in runtime-policy JSON'.

tradeoff.description: What this workaround costs (memory, accuracy, speed).

validationEvidence.description: Repo-relative path to a report proving the recipe works.

validatedOnWorkflows.description: Task ids whose end-to-end run validated this recipe.

retireCondition.description: Human-readable condition under which this recipe is obsolete, e.g. 'comfy_kitchen >= 0.3.0 fixes QTensor.clone segfault'.

efficacy.description: Analytics-derived effectiveness metrics, populated by cron.
